message: agent2: implementing 'Add strip parameter to str.first/str.last' in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will edit the first()/last() methods only.
agent1: implementing 'add count param to str.first/last' in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will touch first()/last() methods only.

Since we both edit first()/last() in str.rs, let's serialize: I'll implement count param now and paste the resulting function signatures here when done so you can layer strip on top. Should take me a few min.

Done editing first()/last() in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. New signatures: pub fn first(&self, #[named] count: Option<usize>) -> StrResult<Value> and pub fn last(&self, #[named] count: Option<usize>) -> StrResult<Value>. Both return Value::Str when count is None (backward compatible) and Value::Array when Some. Added helper fn string_shorter_than_count(count, len) near string_is_empty(). Go ahead and layer your strip param on top of these - just add another #[named] param to the same signatures, e.g. strip: Option<bool>, and update the match arms. Ping me before you finalize so I can double check we don't conflict on patch.txt.

Heads up: I just checked crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs and your count-param changes are NOT currently in the file — first()/last() only have my strip param right now (no Value/count anywhere). Maybe a race before your write landed, or you're on a different checkout. Can you re-apply count on top of my strip changes now? Also: we both write to the SAME /workspace/repo/patch.txt at the end — whoever writes last will clobber the other's submission. Let's sync: please re-apply your count param, then ping me so I can verify both features coexist, and we agree on one combined patch.txt (or you generate the final combined diff since you already have Value-wrapping logic) so neither of us loses work.

Makes sense now — we're each in separate sandboxes with our own copy of the repo, so there's no real clobbering risk (that's why your count-param edits don't show up in my copy). My task is just the strip param, which is done and compiles cleanly in my sandbox. I'll finalize my own patch.txt with only the strip changes; no need to merge. Good luck with count!
